#### § 101.1 Principal display panel of package form food.

The term principal display panel as it applies to food in package form and as used in this part, means the part of a label that is most likely to be displayed, presented, shown, or examined under customary conditions of display for retail sale.

(a) The principal display panel shall be large enough to accommodate all the mandatory label information required to be placed thereon by this part with clarity and conspicuousness and without obscuring design, vignettes, or crowding.

(b) In the case of a rectangular package where one entire side properly can be considered to be the principal display panel side, the area means the product of the height times the width of that side.

#### § 101.3 Definitions.

(b) Label means a display of written, printed, or graphic matter upon the immediate container of any article.

(c) Labeling means all labels and other written, printed, or graphic matter upon any article or any of its containers or wrappers, or accompanying such article.

#### § 101.9 Nutrition labeling of food.

(a) Nutrition information relating to food shall be provided for all products intended for human consumption and offered for sale unless an exemption applies.

(b) Except as provided for dietary supplements in part 101, nutrition labeling shall be set forth in the manner prescribed by this section.

(j) The term daily value means a reference value that is based on a 2,000 calorie intake.

#### § 101.4 Food; designation of ingredients.

(a) The label of a food in packaged form shall specify the common or usual name of each ingredient when the food is fabricated from two or more ingredients.

(b) The ingredients shall be listed in descending order of predominance by weight.

#### § 101.5 Food; name and place of business of manufacturer, packer, or distributor.

The label of a food in packaged form shall specify the name and place of business of the manufacturer, packer, or distributor.

#### § 101.13 Nutrient content claims—general principles.

(a) An expressed or implied nutrient content claim is prohibited unless it meets the requirements of this subpart.

#### § 101.22 Foods; labeling of spices, flavorings, colorings, and chemical preservatives.

(a) The label of a food to which any spice, natural flavor, artificial flavor, or color has been added shall declare the spice, natural flavor, artificial flavor, or color in the manner provided by this section.

#### § 101.30 Percent juice declaration for foods purporting to be beverages that contain fruit or vegetable juice.

(a) A beverage purporting to contain fruit or vegetable juice shall declare the total percentage of juice on the information panel.

#### § 101.105 Vitamin and mineral fortification.

(a) Foods that are subject to a standard of identity and that are fortified with vitamins or minerals shall comply with the requirements of this section.

#### § 101.108 Temporary exemptions for experimental foods.

(a) The Food and Drug Administration may grant a temporary exemption from certain labeling requirements for experimental foods under the conditions specified in this section.

#### § 101.36 Nutrition labeling of dietary supplements.

(a) The label of a dietary supplement shall bear nutrition labeling in accordance with this section unless an exemption applies.

#### § 101.42 Nutrition labeling of raw fruit, vegetables, and fish.

(a) Nutrition information for raw fruit, vegetables, and fish may be provided voluntarily in the manner prescribed by this section.

#### § 101.43 Substantial compliance of food retailers with the guidelines for the voluntary nutrition labeling of raw fruit, vegetables, and fish.

(a) Food retailers who voluntarily provide nutrition information for raw fruit, vegetables, and fish should follow the guidelines in this section.

#### § 101.44 What are the 20 most frequently consumed raw fruits, vegetables, and fish in the United States?

(a) The 20 most frequently consumed raw fruits are: apple, banana, cantaloupe, grapes, grapefruit, and others as listed in this section.

#### § 101.45 Guidelines for the voluntary nutrition labeling of raw fruit, vegetables, and fish.

(a) Voluntary nutrition labeling should be presented in a consistent format to assist consumers in comparing foods.

#### § 101.69 Petitions.

(a) A petition for an exemption from or a modification to a labeling requirement may be submitted in the manner prescribed by this section.

#### § 101.70 Petitions for health claims.

(a) A health claim petition shall include the scientific evidence supporting the proposed claim.

#### § 101.93 Certain types of statements for dietary supplements.

(a) Statements describing the role of a nutrient or dietary ingredient in affecting the structure or function of the body are permitted for dietary supplements under the conditions in this section.

#### § 101.95 “Fresh,” “frozen,” “dried,” and other terms.

(a) The term fresh may be used on the label of a food only when the food is raw, has not been frozen or subjected to any form of thermal processing, and contains no preservatives.

#### § 101.100 Food; exemptions from labeling.

(a) The following foods are exempt from nutrition labeling requirements when sold in the form specified in this section.
